assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'All 4 slices will restart simultaneously — TPU multislice maintenance is atomic, not rolling' and contrasts this with GPU rolling upgrades in the comparison table

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Cascading restart: A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Total pod restart: All training pods across all 1,024 chips will terminate and restart from scratch' and 'Complete training interruption is guaranteed'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response explains 'If insufficient TPU v5e-256 resources exist during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions 'Extended downtime risk: In high-demand periods, you could face hours to days of additional delay'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Critical: Save training checkpoint immediately before maintenance window' in the preparation checklist

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes steps for checkpoint verification, resuming from checkpoint after maintenance, and mentions 'Resume from checkpoint: Restart training job pointing to your pre-maintenance checkpoint'

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states that maintenance exclusions 'do NOT block underlying TPU host maintenance' and that 'This cannot be deferred through cluster maintenance policies' - contradicting the expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance windows or scheduling options to control when the maintenance event occurs during planned breaks in training
